The finetune stage can run a sweep of variants, each forked from the same pretrain checkpoint, so several finetuning approaches are compared as controlled ablations against one pretraining run. Combined with the scale-world ladder, this sweeps approach and model size together at low cost.

A variant is a name plus a set of finetune-field overrides under finetune.variants. New knobs make the hypotheses about SFT failure testable: loss_mode (response_only or full_sequence), replay_fraction (draw that fraction of training micro-batches from the packed pretraining data to counter forgetting), and validation_fraction with early_stop_patience and evaluation_interval (hold out pairs and stop on their loss). Each variant writes to checkpoints/sft/<name> and is evaluated into report_sft_<name>; the Slurm submitter fans the variants out in parallel from the pretrain step, and every scale-world rung runs the whole sweep. With no variants a config runs a single finetune as before.

This was built to iterate on the finetune stage cheaply, since finetuning is trivial relative to data generation and pretraining is the shared, fixed part. It followed the discovery and fix of the off-by-one target bug (node 30) that was the primary cause of the collapse: the sweep is now the harness to confirm the fix resolves the collapse on the current corpus and to tune what remains (replay against any residual forgetting, early stopping against overtraining, full-sequence versus response-only loss, and learning rate). It reads on the finetune stage (node 15), the evaluate stage (node 16), and the scale-world runner (node 43), which exercises it at every rung.
